figures.caption: Figure 1. Numerical configuration of the annular duct. The left figure illustrates the cross-section of the duct, while the right figure provides an overview of the computational domain. The contours of axial velocity are shown within the volume domain, and the density contours are displayed on the inner and outer walls.

Figure 2. Near-wall fluid velocity statistics: (𝑎) mean axial fluid velocity 𝑢+𝑧 , (𝑏) axial rms fluid velocity fluctuation 𝑢′+𝑧,rms, (𝑐) radial rms fluid velocity fluctuation 𝑢′+𝑟 ,rms and (𝑑) azimuthal rms fluid velocity fluctuation 𝑢′+ 𝜃,rms. The superscript + denotes viscous scaling, and 𝑟+ denotes the wall-normal distance in wall units. The markers denote the data from Kim et al. (1987) (plane channel, Ref = 3300), the UUD dataset of Marchioli et al. (2008) (plane channel, Ref = 2100) and the data from Chung et al. (2002) (concentric annuli, Ref = 2225).
Table 2. Parameters of the different cases. Here, 𝑁p denotes the total particle number, 𝛷p denotes the particle volume fraction, and 𝜂w denotes the smallest eddy scale near the wall.

Figure 3. Near-wall particle velocity statistics: (𝑎) mean axial particle velocity 𝑣+𝑧 , (𝑏) axial rms particle velocity fluctuation 𝑣′+𝑧,rms, (𝑐) radial rms particle velocity fluctuation 𝑣′+𝑟 ,rms and (𝑑) azimuthal rms particle velocity fluctuation 𝑣′+ 𝜃,rms. The superscript + denotes viscous scaling, and 𝑟+ denotes the wall-normal distance in wall units. The markers denote the UUD dataset of Marchioli et al. (2008) (plane channel, Ref = 2100).

Figure 5. Spatial and temporal evolution of the dimensionless particle concentration 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡) near the (outer) wall of (𝑎) case 1, (𝑏) case 2, (𝑐) case 3, (𝑑) case 4, (𝑒) case 5 and ( 𝑓 ) case 6. Here, 𝑟+ denotes the viscous-scaled radial distance, and contours represent the particle concentration 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡), with black iso-lines denoting 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡) = 1.

Figure 6. Spatial and temporal evolution of the dimensionless particle concentration 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡) near the (inner) wall of (𝑎) case 1, (𝑏) case 2, (𝑐) case 3, (𝑑) case 4, (𝑒) case 5 and ( 𝑓 ) case 6. Here, 𝑟+ denotes the viscous-scaled radial distance, and contours represent the particle concentration 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡), with black iso-lines denoting 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡) = 1.
Figure 7. Dimensionless mean particle concentration within the viscous sublayer (𝑟+ < 5) near the (𝑎) outer wall and (𝑏) inner wall.

Figure 8. (𝑎) Radial profiles of the steady-state concentration and contributions of phoresis integrals: (𝑏) biased sampling integral 𝐼𝑏 and a zoomed-in view of its variations near the outer wall, (𝑐) turbophoresis integral 𝐼𝑡 and a zoomed-in view of its variations near the outer wall, (𝑑) centrifugal integral 𝐼𝑐 and a zoomed-in view of 𝐼𝑡 + 𝐼𝑐 , where the black dashed line marks the zero level.

Figure 9. Temporal evolution of the short-time-averaged particle-sampled velocity quantities associated with 𝐷1 and 𝐷2: radial fluid velocity ⟨𝑢𝑟 |𝑟⟩ (solid lines), radial rms particle velocity ⟨𝑣2 𝑟 |𝑟⟩1/2 (dashed lines) and azimuthal rms particle velocity ⟨𝑣2 𝜃 |𝑟⟩1/2 (dotted lines) for (𝑎) case 1, (𝑏) case 2, (𝑐) case 3, (𝑑)

Figure 10. Characteristics of the modes: (𝑎) the time scales normalized by 𝜏p of the slowly decaying modes (circle), rapidly decaying modes (square), and oscillatory decaying modes (cross); (𝑏) the initial modal energy contribution 𝐸𝑘,𝑛/𝐸𝑘 ; (𝑐) the cumulative modal energy contribution ∑𝑛 𝑖=0 𝐸𝑘,𝑖/𝐸𝑘 ; (𝑑) the relative amplitudes of slowly decaying modes 𝛼𝑛 = |𝑎𝑛 |/( |𝑎𝑛 | + |𝑏𝑛 |) (circle) and rapidly decaying modes 𝛽𝑛 = |𝑏𝑛 |/( |𝑎𝑛 | + |𝑏𝑛 |) (square).
Figure 11. Radial profile of initial flux 𝐽+𝑛 (𝑟, 0) for (𝑎) the outer-wall transport mode and (𝑏) the wallward transport mode.
Figure 12. Reconstructed near-wall concentration 𝑐w for (𝑎) case 1, (𝑏) case 2, (𝑐) case 3, (𝑑) case 4, (𝑒) case 5 and ( 𝑓 ) case 6.
Figure 13. Profiles of the (𝑎) mean temperature 𝑇f and density 𝜌f and (𝑏) rms of the corresponding fluctuations 𝑇 ′ f,rms and 𝜌′f,rms.

Figure 14. Spatial and temporal evolution of the dimensionless particle concentration 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡) near the (𝑎) outer and (𝑏) inner walls for case 7, and (𝑐, 𝑑) the corresponding dimensionless mean particle concentration within the viscous sublayer (𝑟+ < 5). Here, 𝑟+ denotes the viscous-scaled radial distance, and contours represent the particle concentration 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡), with black iso-lines denoting 𝑐(𝑟, 𝑡) = 1. A zoomed-in view of inner-wall concentration 𝑐w is added in figure 14(𝑑).

Figure 15. Characteristics of the modes: (𝑎) the time scales normalized by 𝜏p of the slowly decaying modes (circle), rapidly decaying modes (square), and oscillatory decaying modes (cross); (𝑏) the initial modal energy contribution 𝐸𝑘,𝑛/𝐸𝑘 ; (𝑐) the cumulative modal energy contribution ∑𝑛 𝑖=0 𝐸𝑘,𝑖/𝐸𝑘 ; (𝑑) the relative amplitudes of slowly decaying modes 𝛼𝑛 = |𝑎𝑛 |/( |𝑎𝑛 | + |𝑏𝑛 |) (circle) and rapidly decaying modes 𝛽𝑛 = |𝑏𝑛 |/( |𝑎𝑛 | + |𝑏𝑛 |) (square).
Figure 16. Radial profile of initial flux 𝐽+𝑛 (𝑟, 0) for (𝑎) the outer-wall transport mode and (𝑏) the wallward transport mode.
